title: Alangium bogoriense

intro: Alangium bogoriense is a species of flowering plant in the family Cornaceae, commonly known as the Alangium tree. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine, particularly in Indonesia and other parts of Southeast Asia, for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties. The bioactive constituents of Alangium bogoriense include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Alangium bogoriense is often prepared as a decoction or a tincture, and is used to treat various ailments, including fever, cough, and skin infections, as well as to promote digestion and relieve joint pain.
